items.text: Start Kajabi free trial (30 days) at kajabi.com

subtasks.text: Go to kajabi.com and click 'Start Free Trial'
Enter your name, email (use ann@yogaismedicine.com), and create a password
Select 'Coaching or Courses' as your business type
Skip the credit card step if prompted — you have 30 days free
Confirm your email address via the verification link Kajabi sends you

items.text: Complete Kajabi onboarding wizard

subtasks.text: Step 1 — Business name: enter 'Yoga Is Medicine'
Step 2 — What do you sell: select 'Online Courses'
Step 3 — Your goal: select 'Grow my audience' or 'Make my first sale'
Step 4 — Upload your logo (use your existing Yoga Is Medicine logo file)
Step 5 — Choose your website theme: select the cleanest/simplest option — you will customize colors next
Step 6 — Set your brand colors: Primary #52796F, Secondary #354F52, Accent #84A89C, Background #F7F5F2, Text #2F3E46
Step 7 — Skip the 'Create your first product' step for now — click 'I'll do this later'
Step 8 — Confirm your time zone is set to your local time (important for email scheduling)
Click 'Go to my dashboard' — your Kajabi home base is now set up

items.text: Upload your logo and set brand colors (#CAD2C5, #84A89C, #52796F, #354F52, #2F3E46)

subtasks.text: In Kajabi dashboard: go to Settings → Appearance
Upload your logo file (PNG with transparent background works best)
Set Primary color: #52796F
Set Secondary color: #354F52
Set Accent color: #84A89C
Save changes and preview your site to confirm colors look correct

items.text: Set up your custom domain (e.g., learn.yogaismedicine.com)

subtasks.text: In Kajabi: go to Settings → Domains
Click 'Add Custom Domain'
Enter: learn.yogaismedicine.com
Kajabi will give you DNS records — copy them
Log into your domain registrar (wherever yogaismedicine.com is registered)
Add the CNAME record Kajabi provided to your DNS settings
Return to Kajabi and click 'Verify' — DNS can take up to 48 hours to propagate

items.text: Connect your Stripe account to Kajabi for payment processing

subtasks.text: In Kajabi: go to Settings → Payments
Click 'Connect with Stripe'
If you don't have a Stripe account: go to stripe.com and create one (free, takes 5 minutes)
Authorize Kajabi to connect to your Stripe account
Set your currency to USD
Test the connection by creating a $0 test product and running a test checkout

items.text: Set up your Kajabi email sender address

subtasks.text: In Kajabi: go to Settings → Email
Set 'From Name' to: Ann Chrapkiewicz
Set 'From Email' to: ann@yogaismedicine.com
Verify your email domain — Kajabi will send a verification link
Add the SPF and DKIM records Kajabi provides to your domain DNS (improves email deliverability)

items.text: Review Kajabi's email and pipeline tools vs. Mailchimp

subtasks.text: In Kajabi: click 'Email' in the left sidebar — explore the broadcast and sequence options
In Kajabi: click 'Pipelines' — this is Kajabi's funnel builder (replaces Unbounce for funnel pages)
Decision: keep Mailchimp for your 2,329-subscriber broadcast list for now
Use Kajabi email only for automated sequences triggered by course purchases or lead magnet downloads
Note your decision in a simple doc: 'Mailchimp = broadcast to full list. Kajabi = automated sequences for buyers.'

items.text: List every tool you currently pay for: Mailchimp, Unbounce, Zapier, Vimeo, website hosting

subtasks.text: Open your bank or credit card statement and find all recurring subscriptions
List each tool, its monthly cost, and what you use it for
Total your current monthly tech spend — this is your baseline

items.text: Decide which tools Kajabi replaces (landing pages, course hosting, basic email sequences)
Keep Mailchimp for your main broadcast list (2,329 subscribers) — do NOT migrate yet
Keep Vimeo for class recordings — Kajabi embeds Vimeo videos directly
Evaluate Unbounce: Kajabi's landing pages may replace it for funnel pages
Set up Zapier: new Kajabi purchase → add 'customer' tag in Mailchimp

subtasks.text: Go to zapier.com and log in (or create a free account)
Click 'Create Zap'
Trigger: Kajabi → New Purchase
Action: Mailchimp → Add/Update Subscriber → add tag 'customer'
Test the Zap with a test purchase
Turn the Zap on

items.text: Document your final tech stack decision in a one-page reference doc
List all your existing Vimeo classes by category (beginner, intermediate, therapeutic, etc.)
Identify your top 10–15 most-watched or most-requested classes — these become your first course
Note your unique methodologies: Internal Literacy, fascia/connective tissue work, Gil Hedley framework, Mary Jarvis Shapeshifting
Draft a one-paragraph 'unique teaching philosophy' statement for your About/Sales pages
Identify 3–5 student transformation stories to use as testimonials in your funnel
Copyright note: your class recordings, written materials, and methodology descriptions are automatically protected
Choose your lead magnet format: PDF guide, mini video series, or short workshop recording
Recommended title: 'The 5 Postures That Changed Everything: A Guide to Internal Literacy'
Write the lead magnet content (5–8 pages): your unique framework, 5 key postures, what to feel for internally
Design the PDF cover in Canva using brand colors and your photo
Upload the finished PDF to Kajabi as a free product
Create the opt-in landing page in Kajabi: headline, 3 bullet points, your photo, opt-in form
Test the opt-in form — confirm the PDF delivers automatically via email
Course title: 'Internal Literacy: A 4-Week Introduction to Feeling Your Practice From the Inside Out'
Outline 4 modules: Week 1 — The Spine, Week 2 — The Hips, Week 3 — The Shoulders, Week 4 — Integration
Select 3–4 existing Vimeo class recordings per module that best illustrate each theme
Record a short intro video for each module (5–10 min) — just you talking to camera
Write a brief 'What You'll Feel' description for each class in the module
Build the course in Kajabi: create product, add modules, embed Vimeo videos
Set price at $47 — test checkout flow with a $0 coupon code
Write the sales page: headline, your story, what's included, transformation promise, testimonials, price, FAQ
Course title: 'Yoga Is Medicine: The Complete Internal Literacy Training'
Outline 8–12 modules covering your full methodology: anatomy, fascia, nervous system, therapeutic applications
This course does NOT need to be complete before launch — build it as students enroll (cohort model)
Set founding member price: $297 for first 20 students, then $497 regular
Write a one-page course overview document for sales conversations and emails
Decide on delivery format: self-paced, live cohort, or hybrid (recommend: live cohort for first round)
Free content schedule: 2 emails/month to full list, 3 Instagram posts/week, 1 YouTube video/month
Email topics bank: Internal Literacy series, Gil Hedley anatomy insights, student transformation stories, posture deep-dives
Instagram content pillars: Teaching clips, Anatomy education, Student stories, Behind-the-scenes, Philosophy/quotes
YouTube strategy: longer-form 'why' videos — 'Why We Lock the Knee,' 'What Fascia Actually Is'
Batch content creation: dedicate one day/month to filming 4–6 short videos and writing 2 emails
Repurpose rule: every email → 2 Instagram captions; every YouTube video → 3 short clips
✅ COMPLETE: Email 1 drafted, photos added, test sent to ann@yogaismedicine.com
Review the test email in your inbox — confirm photos display correctly
Log into Mailchimp → Campaigns → 'Email 1 — Re-Engagement (Restored Draft)'
Click Schedule or Send — recommended: Tuesday or Wednesday, 9–11am
After sending: monitor open rate (goal: 25%+) and reply rate (goal: 5+ replies)
Subject line: 'The thing I've been trying to teach for 20 years has a name'
Opening: Reference replies from Email 1 — 'So many of you wrote back, and I read every single one.'
Body: Introduce the concept of Internal Literacy — what it means, why it changes everything
Include one specific example: a posture (e.g., Standing Bow) and what you're asking students to feel internally
Photo: Use the Colorado mountain portrait (close-up) — warm, thoughtful, personal
Soft CTA: 'Reply and tell me — what posture do you most want to understand from the inside out?'
P.S.: Mention you're working on a guide — plant the seed for the lead magnet
Subject line: 'I made you something (it's free)'
Body: Announce the free guide — '5 Postures That Changed Everything'
Explain what's inside: what they'll learn, what they'll feel, why it matters
Clear CTA button: 'Download Your Free Guide'
Link to your Kajabi opt-in landing page
Note: this email goes to your full Mailchimp list of 2,329 — it's your first funnel entry point
Email 4 (Day 1 after download): 'Did you get a chance to try it?' — check in, answer questions
Email 5 (Day 3): Deep dive on one concept from the guide — build authority and connection
Email 6 (Day 5): Student transformation story — someone whose practice changed through this approach
Email 7 (Day 7): Soft introduction to the mini-course — 'If you want to go deeper, I've built something for you'
Set these 4 emails up as an automated sequence in Kajabi (triggered by lead magnet download)
Email 8: Launch announcement — 'It's here. Internal Literacy: 4 Weeks to Feeling Your Practice'
Email 9 (Day 2): What's inside — module breakdown, video count, what they'll feel by Week 4
Email 10 (Day 4): FAQ email — answer the 5 most common questions/objections
Email 11 (Day 6): Testimonial email — student story, before/after, specific transformation
Email 12 (Day 7): Last chance — cart closes tonight, personal note from Ann
Add a 'Free Guide' banner or popup to yogaismedicine.com linking to your Kajabi opt-in page
Add an 'Online Courses' page or nav item to your website — link to your Kajabi storefront
Update your About page bio to mention your online offerings
Add the Colorado mountain portrait as your new bio photo on the website
Ensure your website footer has links to: Free Guide, Mini-Course, Email List signup
Instagram: announce the free guide with a Reel — film yourself explaining Internal Literacy in 60 seconds
Instagram bio: update link to your Kajabi opt-in page (or use Linktree with guide + course links)
Facebook: share the same Reel + a longer written post about your teaching philosophy
YouTube: record a 10-minute 'Introduction to Internal Literacy' video — evergreen traffic
Pin the free guide announcement to the top of your Instagram and Facebook profiles
Kajabi pipeline: build a 3-step pipeline — Opt-in page → Thank you page → Email sequence start
Zapier: new Kajabi customer → add tag 'customer' in Mailchimp → remove from prospect sequences
Kajabi: set up affiliate program for students who want to refer others (10–20% commission)
Create a 'Welcome' automated email for new Mailchimp subscribers who haven't yet downloaded the guide
Set a monthly calendar reminder: review Kajabi analytics, email open rates, and course enrollment numbers
Set a quarterly reminder: update the lead magnet with new insights, refresh email sequences
Month 1 goal: 50 lead magnet downloads, 5 mini-course sales ($235)
Month 3 goal: 200 downloads, 20 mini-course sales ($940), 2 signature course founding members ($594)
Month 6 goal: 500 downloads, 50 mini-course sales ($2,350), 10 signature course students ($2,970)
Month 12 goal: 1,000+ downloads, 100 mini-course sales/year ($4,700), 30 signature course students ($8,910) = ~$13,610/year passive
Track weekly: new subscribers, lead magnet downloads, course sales, email open rates
Celebrate every milestone — this is a practice, not a sprint. Each step compounds.
